title: Docker-compat REST API omits `HostConfig` and returns `NetworkSettings.Networks = null`
body:
### Issue Description

When Podman’s Docker-compatible socket is enabled (`systemctl --now podman.socket`), a container is not represented the way Docker represents the same container with regard to networks.
The fields that many Docker-based service discovery mechanisms rely on -- `HostConfig.NetworkMode` and `NetworkSettings.Networks` -- are missing or null.

**Impact**
Software that scrape Docker metadata for service discovery -- e.g. VictoriaMetrics’ `docker_sd_configs` (https://github.com/VictoriaMetrics/VictoriaMetrics/blob/bb54075c23dd89c6fb8f120fd83bcb1c27ac8854/lib/promscrape/discovery/docker/container.go#L120) -- often depends on these fields and fail to recognize other containers and in the case of VictoriaMetircs fail to scrape their metrics.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```bash
# 1. Start a container
podman run -d <image>

# 2. Query the Docker-compat API
curl --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock \
     http://d/containers/json | jq '.[0] | {HostConfig,NetworkSettings}'
```


### Describe the results you received

**Actual output (trimmed)**

```json
{
  "HostConfig": null,
  "NetworkSettings": { "Networks": null }
}
```


### Describe the results you expected

**Expected output (reference taken from Docker 28.2.2)**

```
$ curl --unix-socket /var/run/docker.sock http://d/containers/json \
  jq '.[0] | {HostConfig,NetworkSettings}'
```

```json
{
  "HostConfig": { "NetworkMode": "host" },
  "NetworkSettings": {
    "Networks": {
      "host": { /* driver-specific details */ }
    }
  }
}
```


### podman info output

```yaml
version:
  APIVersion: 4.3.1
  Built: 0
  BuiltTime: Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.19.8
  Os: linux
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 4.3.1
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Both

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

Initially I thought it is constrained to when a container is run with `--network=host` but it turned out it happens regardless of it so I updated the description accordingly.

comments.body: Hey @atodekangae , I will take a look into this issue !
4.3.1 is a very old Podman. Any chance you can try with 5.5.1? I suspect some of this has been improved over the last two years.

I tried Podman 5.5.1 and I confirmed that `/containers/json` responds with `.NetworkSettings` filled:

```
$ curl -s --unix-socket /var/run/user/1001/podman/podman.sock http://d/containers/json | jq '.[0] | {HostConfig, Networ
kSettings}'
{
  "HostConfig": null,
  "NetworkSettings": {
    "Networks": {
      "host": {
        "IPAMConfig": null,
        "Links": null,
        "Aliases": null,
        "MacAddress": "",
        "DriverOpts": null,
        "GwPriority": 0,
        "NetworkID": "host",
        "EndpointID": "",
        "Gateway": "",
        "IPAddress": "",
        "IPPrefixLen": 0,
        "IPv6Gateway": "",
        "GlobalIPv6Address": "",
        "GlobalIPv6PrefixLen": 0,
        "DNSNames": null
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Although `.HostConfig` is set to `null`, which is different from Docker's response, the application that did not work with the older Podman (VictoriaMetrics) seems to work fine.

[restful api](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/26840)

here i can reproduce your issue. when use http://d/vxxx/libpod/container/create , not combine network interface. when use http://libpod/container/create will combine network but error container name..
I think I'm going to close this one given that the original `.NetworkSettings` issue seems resolved. We can continue in the linked issue if there are further problems.
